lineage.method: Bayesian-IR shrunk weights on the 11 lsk3 score factors, in-sample window = first 60% of panel timestamps (2023-04 to ~2025-04), tau_t=2.0, ic_to_weight_scale=3.25.

thesis_profile.market_mechanism: W3.6 v3 score: identical 11 factors to v_alpha_v1_lsk3 but weights are Bayesian-IR-shrunk from the first 60% of panel timestamps (rather than hand-tuned from full-history IC). The in-sample weight estimation uses prior IC=0 with tau_t=2.0 (factors with |t-stat|<2 get heavily shrunk toward 0); weight = posterior_IC * 3.25 (matching v91's |w|/|IC| ratio). Stacks with the W3.5 v2 regime-gating overlay (alpha_ontology_regime_gating_v2) for the gated portfolio sizing layer. Hypothesis: hand-tuned weights inadvertently over-allocate to factors with strong magnitude but weak in-sample t-stats (e.g. distance_to_high_60 at +0.18 with t=2.25); shrinking those toward the posterior should reduce overfit and improve walk-forward robustness, especially in regimes where the over-weighted factor under-performs.
thesis_profile.directional_claim: Same long-short top-3 perp signed-z-score construction. Score uses Bayesian-IR-shrunk weights instead of hand-tuned. Position size multiplied by W3.5 v2 regime-gating overlay. Falsified if (a) walk_forward median sharpe drops more than 0.5 below v_alpha_v1_lsk3_g_v2 (+2.147) AND worst-regime fails to improve relative to v1_lsk3_g_v2 (-1.851); OR (b) any new strict gate fails (regime_holdout, walk_forward, factor_evidence, execution_stress).
thesis_profile.factor_formula: raw = sum_i (weight_v3_i * z(factor_i)) where weight_v3_i is loaded from config/quant_research/alpha_ontology_v3_weights.json (Bayesian shrinkage, in-sample first 60% of panel, tau_t=2, scale 3.25). final_score = tanh((percentile_rank(raw)-0.5)*1.80); target_weight_per_name = (long_leverage/top_K - short_leverage/bottom_K) * regime_gating_v2_multiplier(t)

verified_outcome_2026_04_29.interpretation: Bayesian-IR shrinkage reduced reliance on factors with strong hand-tuned weight but weak in-sample t-stat (notably distance_to_high_60: hand 0.18, t=2.25, v3 0.04). That cost peak trend-regime alpha (+5.54 -> +3.88) but flipped the previously-negative drawdown_rebound_2026ytd regime to strongly positive (-1.85 -> +1.81), giving v3 a more balanced 2/3 positive_regime_fraction. v3's walk-forward median (+1.87) is below v1_lsk3_g_v2's +2.15 because the trend regime carries less peak alpha; in exchange v3 has the lowest worst regime among gated variants (-1.612 vs lsk3_g_v2 -1.851). The two are sibling strict-passing variants with different risk profiles: lsk3_g_v2 = max walk-forward peak; v3_lsk3_g_v2 = max regime breadth + worst-regime safety.

active_alternative_reason: W3.6 v3 score (Bayesian-IR-shrunk weights) + W3.5 v2 overlay = the structural three-layer architecture from doc H.2 (score factors + Bayesian-IR weighting + gating multipliers). Strict-passes v10 contract with positive_regime_fraction 2/3 (better than lsk3_g_v2's 1/3) and worst regime -1.612 (better than lsk3_g_v2's -1.851). Walk-forward median (+1.870) is below lsk3_g_v2 (+2.147) but well above un-gated lsk3 (+1.748). v3_lsk3_g_v2 is the active_alternative when operator wants regime breadth and worst-regime safety; lsk3_g_v2 stays active_alternative when operator wants max walk-forward peak.
